fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 66 is inverted: `if not clamp` applies clamping when clamp is False, and skips clamping when clamp is True. It should be `if clamp:` to clamp when clamping is requested.

spec.expr: (clamp and (y__1 < min(y0, y1) or y__1 > max(y0, y1))) or (not clamp)

constraints.intent: When clamp is True and interpolated value is out of range, the clamping block should be entered; the inverted condition prevents this.
The then-branch of the if-not-clamp block should only execute when clamp is False; verifies the inverted logic.

spec.expr: (not clamp) or (y__5 >= min(y0, y1) and y__5 <= max(y0, y1))

constraints.intent: When clamping is enabled, the final y value must be within [min(y0,y1), max(y0,y1)].
Before entering the clamping logic, confirm y__1 is correctly interpolated so the clamping branch condition can be evaluated correctly.

spec.expr: (not clamp) or (__cbfl_result >= min(y0, y1) and __cbfl_result <= max(y0, y1))

constraints.intent: When clamp=True, returned value must be within the supply range of the two endpoints.
